behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff: three collectors -> aggregation/composition -> summary input payload. MOCK-MISS: this is not single-function logic; it is wiring/state merge across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required to prove this behavior; in-process source stubs are sufficient. LLM-DEP: behavior is about input to generation, not model judgment/output quality, so Agentic is disqualified by the text "content passed to the summary-generation step". MULTI-STEP: no chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/aggregator modules with three deterministic source adapters (test doubles), execute collection, and assert the exact payload handed to the summary component contains all three source sections with correct mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction: source error propagation + report assembly fallback. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of one function would miss handoff/continuation behavior. REAL-INFRA: can inject one failing adapter in-process; real network outage semantics are not required by behavior text. LLM-DEP: requirement is fallback control path and note insertion, not model output quality; text "data unavailable note ... rather than aborting" disqualifies Agentic. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with two successful source adapters and one adapter that raises an unavailable error; assert run completes, report contains a `data unavailable` marker in the failing section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model judgment/content fidelity. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing generation would always pass structure checks while hiding misrepresentation/hallucination failures. LLM-DEP: explicit quality requirement ("accurately represents") requires real model outputs across runs/prompts. MULTI-STEP: this is a single generation-quality boundary, not a multi-agent pipeline, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and evaluate whether output preserves critical metrics/issues/deploy facts (with rubric/tolerance), repeating enough runs to detect non-deterministic misrepresentation.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is required: real HTTP/API calls and real email delivery/inbox boundary. In-process mocks can hide transport/auth/provider behavior and inbox acceptance. The behavior text explicitly demands end-to-end delivery to a real recipient inbox ("reaches ... inbox correctly"). Agentic is not required here because success criterion is delivery path correctness, not summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end against real reachable source endpoints and real email service in a test account, then verify recipient inbox receives one complete report with expected sections/metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration/state handoff across modules: delivery failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry. Integration can catch this with real module wiring plus controlled failing mail adapter and local filesystem state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network behavior beyond injectable failure; (b) no AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require validating provider-specific outage semantics, only fallback and retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with mail adapter forced unreachable; assert report persisted to local file/spool. Run scheduler-triggered second execution with mail adapter healthy; assert persisted report is retried/sent and local pending artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
